open_hooks_to_carry: The sub-Ledger's silence: the Ledger's background hum is gone — the absence is physical, like a phantom limb in reverse

The sub-Ledger as a specific civilization: medicine with obsidian-edge tools and herbalism apprenticeships; governance by council with real tensions between voluntary and involuntary zeros; architecture of hand-quarried stone showing tool marks
Vel finding Maren: a reunion that is complex — eight years apart, Maren has built a life, neither is the person who left; but Vel is there and Maren is there
Kem in a world without the Ledger: just hands, no stats, something close to peace
Kem's House signet set on a windowsill — just a ring, meaning nothing down here
The threat: the Hoarded know about the threshold now — the Reserve witnessed the crossing

Intercut — Sable at 229 at the threshold's edge: she chooses to file a false report ('Level-zero termination. No threshold event observed.'), protecting the sub-Ledger with a lie that won't hold forever
Book 1 ending: everything to protect, nothing left to spend

payoffs_to_land.description: The House signet reaches its final position: set on a windowsill in the sub-Ledger, stripped of meaning — Kem's identity transformation rendered through an object's journey from ceremonial stand to pocket to windowsill

Kem's Ch 14 flash of comprehension — understanding why someone might choose preservation — resonates in the sub-Ledger: he chose embodiment, and now he lives with the full weight of that choice in a world that demands everything from the body

prev_tail_excerpt:
The commander turned to her. "Auditor."

"I am —" Sable stopped. "Do not say anything right now."

She did not look at the commander again. She looked through the opening at hand-cut stone, packed earth, and people moving under that silent sky.

"Go," Vel said.

Kem spent his last two levels.

There was no flare to it. Just the final shove of energy into a failing mechanism and then the clean blankness that took his face when the Ledger left him.

He crossed.

He hit the packed earth stumbling. Vel caught him with her good arm. He hissed around the bad rib and almost went down anyway.

"Hey," Vel said.

He tried to laugh. The rib turned it into a hiss. "We — yeah."

Behind him, Sable stood on the basin floor at 229, hands lowered, wind dragging at her ruined hair. She stayed where she was while the agents regrouped. The commander looked from Sable to the place where the tear had been and said nothing.

The threshold folded in on itself. The Scour vanished. Silence stayed.

Somewhere in the stone streets behind Vel, a voice called out. Then another, closer. Footsteps started toward them.

Vel kept one hand on Kem's arm and looked toward the nearest turn in the street.

She waited for Maren to come around it.
state_deltas_required: The weight of arrival; the fragility of the sub-Ledger's secrecy now that the crossing has been witnessed; the political complexity of the world below

The destination reached; the sub-Ledger rendered as a specific, politically complex civilization; personal quests resolve imperfectly; Sable protects the sub-Ledger with a lie; the saga question opens
